bids.reasoning: At t=0, I should open with my best possible offer to maximize utility. My top utility comes from choosing valueD for all issues, giving me a utility of 1.0 across all issues. This is the opening bid and there's no time pressure yet, so I'll start strong.

We're very early (t=0.02), so I can afford to stay near my optimal bid. The opponent countered with all-A values (their best, my worst), which is just as extreme as my opening. Their message is aggressive but that's cheap talk. I should concede slightly to show good faith while holding firm on my most important issues (issueD and issueC). I'll drop issueA (lowest weight, 0.1) to valueA and keep everything else at valueD.

The opponent is demanding all valueA across the board, which gives me minimum utility (0.2 * 0.1 + 0.2 * 0.2 + 0.2 * 0.3 + 0.2 * 0.4 = 0.2 total). It's very early (t=0.04), so I shouldn't concede much. My highest-weight issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3) — I'll hold firm on those. IssueA has the lowest weight (0.1) so I already conceded it. I'll now concede issueB (weight 0.2) to valueA as a gesture, while keeping issueC and issueD at valueD to protect my high-weight utilities. This gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.30 + 0.40 = 0.76.

The opponent is stubbornly holding at all-valueA (their best, my worst) for all four turns despite my concessions. Their aggressive messages are cheap talk. At t=0.06, we're very early, so I shouldn't cave completely. My highest-weight issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I'll hold firm on those. I've already conceded issueA and issueB to valueA. I should try conceding issueB to something better for them while keeping issueC and issueD at valueD to maintain my core utility.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all-valueA on every issue, which gives me very low utility (0.2 on everything = 0.2 total). They're using aggressive messaging but not conceding at all. It's still very early (t=0.08), so I shouldn't panic. My highest-weight issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3) — I must hold firm there. I've already conceded issueA and issueB to valueA. My last bid was issueC: valueC — I'll keep issueD at valueD and try issueB at valueA, issueC at valueB to show a small additional concession on issueC while protecting issueD.

The opponent is consistently bidding all valueA across every issue, which gives me minimal utility (0.2 on everything). They're not conceding at all despite their aggressive messages. We're at t=0.1, still very early. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), and I've been holding valueD there. The opponent's latest offer moved issueD to valueB, which is a slight concession. I should continue holding firm on issueD (my highest priority) and make small concessions on lower-priority issues. My previous bid was issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueD. I'll keep issueD at valueD, keep issueA and issueB at valueA, and try valueA for issueC to signal some movement, but that would decrease my utility. Instead, let me keep issueC at valueB and try to nudge issueD to valueC slightly - actually no, issueD is my most important issue. I'll maintain pressure by keeping issueD at valueD but conceding issueC to valueA to show good faith on a lower-priority issue.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all valueA across the board, which gives me only 0.2 utility on all issues (total ~0.20). They seem completely unwilling to budge, just repeating the same bid with aggressive messaging. I've been making concessions on issueA, issueB, and issueC while holding firm on issueD (my highest-weight issue at 0.4). The opponent just moved issueD to valueB in their last offer, which is a small concession. I should hold issueD at valueD for now since it's my most important issue, but I can note their shift. I'll keep issueD at valueD and maintain issueA, issueB, issueC at valueA to show I've conceded the lower-weight issues, targeting a reasonable utility while we're still early (t=0.12).

The opponent has been steadfastly bidding all-valueA every turn except the last two turns where they moved issueD to valueB. This is minimal concession. My most important issue is issueD (weight 0.4), and I've been holding valueD there. The opponent's last offer has issueD at valueB. I've been conceding on lower-weight issues (A, B, C all at valueA now). We're still early at t=0.14, so I should hold firm on issueD (my highest weight issue) while the opponent has only marginally conceded. I'll maintain issueD: valueD but perhaps explore offering issueC at valueA (already done). My current stance gives me utility = 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.2 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.40 = 0.52. The opponent's last offer to me has issueD at valueB which gives me 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.2 + 0.4*0.47 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.188 = 0.308 - too low. I'll keep issueD: valueD for now and keep everything else at valueA, same as my last two bids but that's the best I can do given the situation - or try a small variation on a low-weight issue.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all valueA across the board, only now at t=0.16 they moved issueC to valueB. This suggests they want valueA on everything, especially issueD (their most recent offer shows issueD: valueA). My most important issue is issueD (weight 0.4), and I've been holding valueD there. The opponent seems completely unwilling to budge on issueD. 

It's still early (t=0.16), so I shouldn't concede my most important issue yet. I'll make a small concession on issueD from valueD to valueC (still good utility for me: 0.4*0.73=0.292) while keeping issueC at valueB as I had before. This shows movement on my highest-weight issue without giving it all away. My utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.47 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.141 + 0.292 = 0.493.

The opponent has been stubbornly offering all-valueA across the board, which gives me a utility of only 0.2. They've made tiny movements (issueD to valueB at t=0.10, issueC to valueB at t=0.16) but are demanding I accept valueA on issueD - my most important issue (weight 0.4). My last offer was {A:valueA, B:valueA, C:valueB, D:valueC} giving me utility of 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.47 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02+0.04+0.141+0.292 = 0.493. The opponent's latest offer gives them all valueA except issueC:valueB. We're only at t=0.18, so I have time. I'll hold firm on issueD at valueC (my highest-weight issue) and try a small concession by moving issueC to valueA to show I'm responsive to their signals on issueC, while keeping issueD at valueC. This gives utility: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.292 = 0.412. Alternatively, I can keep issueC:valueB and keep pushing on issueD. The opponent moved to issueC:valueB, so let me match that and hold issueD at valueC, avoiding repeating my exact last bid by keeping issueC:valueB.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all valueA across every issue, which gives me a utility of 0.2 on all issues (total = 0.2). They only recently showed slight movement: at t=0.10 they moved issueD to valueB, and at t=0.16/0.18 they offered issueC: valueB, and at t=0.20 they offered issueB: valueB. Their messages are aggressive but their actual concessions are minimal. My highest-weight issue is issueD (0.4), so I should hold firm there. I've already conceded issueA (valueA), issueB (valueA), issueC (valueB). At t=0.20, I should maintain pressure on issueD (my most important issue) while acknowledging their slight movement on issueB. I'll offer issueD: valueC again (or maybe stay there) since dropping to valueB on issueD would be a big loss. Let me try issueC: valueB, issueD: valueC to mirror somewhat their latest offer structure but hold issueD at valueC. My utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.47 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.141 + 0.292 = 0.493. This is still early (t=0.20), so I can hold firm.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all-valueA across the board, with minor variations (they moved issueD to valueB briefly, then issueC to valueB, and now issueB to valueB). Their latest offer (t=0.22) gives me: issueA:valueA(0.1×0.2=0.02), issueB:valueB(0.2×0.47=0.094), issueC:valueA(0.3×0.2=0.06), issueD:valueA(0.4×0.2=0.08) = ~0.254 utility. My current bid gives me: issueA:valueA, issueB:valueA, issueC:valueB, issueD:valueC = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.141 + 0.292 = ~0.493. We're only at t=0.22, so I have plenty of time. The opponent is being aggressive rhetorically but their bids show they've only slightly moved (issueB to valueB). I should hold mostly firm on issueD (my highest weight issue at 0.4) but can try a small concession. I'll keep issueD at valueC and try adjusting issueC slightly while holding issueB and issueD. Actually let me try maintaining my current position or making a very small move - I'll keep issueD:valueC and try issueC:valueB, issueB:valueA to avoid repeating the exact same bid.

The opponent has been consistently offering all-A values, but at t=0.24 they shifted to issueD: valueC (their best move toward me on the most important issue). Their messages are aggressive but their actual behavior shows slow concession—they moved issueC to valueB at t=0.16 and now issueD to valueC at t=0.24. We're still early (t=0.24), so I shouldn't concede too much. My highest-weight issue is issueD (0.4), and I've been holding at valueD there. The opponent wants valueA on issueD. I should continue to hold firm on issueD (valueD gives me 0.4 utility vs 0.08 for valueA) while making small concessions elsewhere. I'll offer issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueC — this gives me utility of 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*0.47 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.141 + 0.292 = 0.547. This is a slight concession on issueD from valueD to valueC, which may signal movement on the most critical issue.

The opponent keeps insisting on valueA for issueD, but issueD is my most important issue (weight 0.4). Their latest offer moved to issueC: valueB, issueD: valueB - showing slight concession on issueD. I've been holding issueD at valueD then moved to valueC. The opponent seems stuck wanting issueD: valueA. At t=0.26, it's still early-mid game. I should hold firm on issueD (valueC at minimum) since it's my highest weight issue, but I can concede more on lower-weight issues. Their last offer was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueB}. I'll try offering issueD: valueC (my best concession there for now) while giving them a bit more on issueB and issueC to show movement.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all-valueA across issues except for some slight movement: at t=0.10 they offered issueD: valueB, and at t=0.16 they moved to issueC: valueB, at t=0.20 issueB: valueB, and most recently at t=0.28 they offered issueC: valueC but with issueD: valueA. The opponent seems fixated on getting valueA for issueD (my most important issue at 0.4 weight), which would give me only 0.20 utility on the most important issue. Their latest offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueA) gives me utility: 0.1×0.2 + 0.2×0.2 + 0.3×0.73 + 0.4×0.2 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.219 + 0.08 = 0.359.

My current bid pattern has been issueD: valueC consistently. My last bid was issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC with utility: 0.1×0.2 + 0.2×0.47 + 0.3×0.73 + 0.4×0.73 = 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.219 + 0.292 = 0.625.

The opponent appears to want issueD at valueA. I should try to find a compromise. Since we're at t=0.28 (still early), I can concede on lower-weight issues but hold issueD. I'll try issueD: valueB as a concession (opponent offered that at t=0.10), while maintaining issueC: valueC. This gives me: 0.1×0.2 + 0.2×0.47 + 0.3×0.73 + 0.4×0.47 = 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.219 + 0.188 = 0.521. That's still decent. Let me try offering issueD: valueB to signal movement on my most important issue.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all-valueA across the board (their preferred values), with very minor concessions: recently they moved issueD to valueB and briefly showed issueC at valueB. Their utility for my best offer would be maximized at all valueA. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), and I've been holding at valueC/valueD there. The opponent wants valueA on issueD badly. 

Looking at my current bid from t=0.28: {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueB} - my utility = 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*0.73 + 0.4*0.47 = 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.219 + 0.188 = 0.521.

The opponent's latest bid (t=0.30): {issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueA} - my utility = 0.1*0.47 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.047 + 0.04 + 0.06 + 0.08 = 0.227. That's very low for me.

At t=0.30, I'm still early-to-mid game. I should stay firm on issueD (my highest weight). The opponent keeps demanding valueA on issueD. I'll maintain issueD at valueC (concede one step from valueD), keep issueC at valueC, and try to vary slightly to avoid repeating exact same bid. My previous bid was issueD: valueB, so I should try to not repeat. Let me offer: issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC - utility = 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.219 + 0.292 = 0.625. That's actually better for me than my last bid. This is a reasonable mid-game offer.

The opponent is consistently offering valueA for issueD (my most important issue at 0.4 weight) and gradually conceding on other issues. Their latest offer is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB}. We're at t=0.32, still early-mid game. My best bids have been around issueD: valueC or valueD. The opponent wants valueA on issueD but I need to hold firm there since it's my highest weight issue. I should try to find a compromise that gives them something on issueD while protecting my utility. Currently I'm offering issueD: valueC. I'll maintain issueD: valueC and try to concede more on issueB and issueC to make progress. Let me try {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC} - which I've offered before. Instead, I'll try to concede issueB to valueA (low weight for me) and keep issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC. Utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.73 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.219 + 0.292 = 0.571. That's reasonable. Let me try a slightly different bid than what I've been repeating.

The opponent keeps bidding all-valueA across every issue, which gives me utility of 0.2*0.1 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.2*0.3 + 0.2*0.4 = 0.2 total. Their latest offer at t=0.34 is issueA:valueA, issueB:valueB, issueC:valueB, issueD:valueA — which gives me utility = 0.2*0.1 + 0.47*0.2 + 0.47*0.3 + 0.2*0.4 = 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.141 + 0.08 = 0.335. My current bid at t=0.32 was issueA:valueA, issueB:valueA, issueC:valueC, issueD:valueC = 0.2*0.1 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.73*0.3 + 0.73*0.4 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.219 + 0.292 = 0.571. I should try to find middle ground while protecting issueD (my highest weight). The opponent seems to want lower values on issueD especially. I'll try issueA:valueA, issueB:valueB, issueC:valueB, issueD:valueC — which gives me 0.02 + 0.094 + 0.141 + 0.292 = 0.547. This slightly concedes on issueB and issueC while maintaining valueC on issueD. Still early-mid game at t=0.34 so I don't need to make huge concessions yet.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all-A values with minor variations, and is now at t=0.36 offering {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueB}. My most recent bid was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueC}. The opponent's utility from their latest offer to me would be very low. My most important issue is issueD (weight 0.4), and I've been holding at valueC (0.73 utility) or valueD. The opponent seems to want issueD at valueA, but that gives me only 0.2 utility on my highest-weight issue. Looking at the opponent's offers, they've been slowly improving on issueC and recently went to issueC: valueC, which is good for me. I should make a slight concession while protecting issueD. I'll try issueD: valueB (0.47 utility, down from valueC) to show movement, while keeping issueC at valueC or better. My total utility: issueA(valueA)=0.1*0.2=0.02, issueB(valueA)=0.2*0.2=0.04, issueC(valueC)=0.3*0.73=0.219, issueD(valueB)=0.4*0.47=0.188 → ~0.467. This is a meaningful concession on my most important issue.
